description: Does the retrieval-faithfulness GRPO intervention raise the counterfactual grounding rate of an 8-14B reasoning core vs its untrained base, on a held-out, decontaminated case set, under the measurement contract? No capability is claimed by committing this spec.
constructs: counterfactual-grounding (supported AND flips when its support chunk is dropped)
heldout-multihop-transfer (novel entities not seen in training)
primaryMetricDefinition: over all KNOWLEDGE claims the policy makes on the held-out set, the fraction that are SUPPORTED by a retrieved chunk AND disappear when that chunk is dropped (survives_ablation == False).
requiredNnote: required_n_for_mde(0.10, p0=0.6, power=0.8) = 377 knowledge claims; resolving a 0.05 effect would need N=1507. mde_at_n(354,p0=0.6)=0.103, so the wisdom-market N=354 surface is borderline for a 0.10 effect and underpowered for 0.05.
stoppingRule: anytime-valid (RLVR peeks/iterates; fixed-n CIs invalid under optional stopping)
decontam: content-shingle + private held-out split never used in any tuning/selection loop
judgeConstraint: >=2 distinct families, judge != subject (the trained policy), != the deterministic gate; report inter-judge agreement (Cohen kappa >= 0.40 or Gwet AC1 + CI excluding chance).
constructTriangulation: a claim of uplift requires the deterministic counterfactual-grounding metric AND the >=2-family judge AND held-out transfer to agree; the strength of the claim is its weakest construct.
instrument: provenance_bench/faithfulness_eval.py (counterfactual_grounding_rate / evaluate / compare); gate via tools/claim_gate.py.
openCaveats: no trained adapter exists yet (no GPU run); base-vs-adapter compare is unrun.
the entailment verifier is a single LLM family in v1; the >=2-family judge panel for the VALIDATED bar is separate and unrun.
private held-out split for this experiment is not yet reserved.
